title: Complete standard-entry contract
objective: Require every admitted reusable system family to have one complete, industry-aligned, machine-validated public standard entry before later batches.
scope: Standard-entry schema, admitted-family backfill, proposal and scaffolding integration, and negative validation
non_goals: No package API change, new family admission, consumer-specific content, or maturity promotion

checkpoints.title: Define and backfill the standard-entry contract
checkpoints.outcome: A versioned schema and admitted-family records answer classification, applicability, boundaries, deliverables, acceptance, misuse, and bounded relationship questions.
non_goals: No package implementation or new system admission

required_capabilities: Can derive reusable system standards from admitted positive evidence and design strict JSON contracts
qualification_evidence: Positive and negative fixtures covering complete, incomplete, and project-specific entries

acceptance.criterion: The schema requires position, applies_when, does_not_apply_when, deliverables, acceptance, common_misuses, and bounded related_entries.
Every admitted live family validates without product-specific content or popularity-only authority.
Admission, derivation, implementation, compatibility, and release maturity remain separate decisions.

verification.procedure: Run the standard-entry schema against admitted families and incomplete or project-task-shaped negative fixtures.
verification.expected: Complete admitted entries pass and incomplete or project-specific entries fail closed.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Comment /claim SE-01 on issue #67 and freeze the schema plus admitted-family backfill in one bounded branch.

checkpoints.title: Integrate standard entries into proposal and scaffolding routes
checkpoints.outcome: Later proposals, admission, and scaffolding fail closed when the complete standard-entry contract is missing or invalid.
non_goals: No automatic proposal acceptance or package creation

required_capabilities: Can integrate governance schemas into contributor, queue, scaffolding, and validation boundaries
qualification_evidence: End-to-end positive and negative admission fixtures plus independent review

acceptance.criterion: Proposal guidance requests the complete standard-entry facts without creating packages.
Later batch admission and scaffolding reject missing or invalid standard entries.
Public guidance explains when a reusable standard applies and when a project-specific need stays outside the library.

verification.procedure: Run the full repository contract with valid and invalid proposal-to-scaffold fixtures.
verification.expected: Only admitted, complete standard entries can reach the implementation blueprint boundary.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for SE-01 completion, then claim SE-02 in issue #68 and integrate the frozen contract without changing package APIs.

integration.strategy: The maintainer reviews the standard records, negative fixtures, proposal route, and scaffolding boundary before merge.
